measurement: SG per-pellet core-hit fraction inside soda-twinkling-bunny's Hit Rate ▲38.91% windows vs matched HR-off windows in the SAME fight — the pre-registered CONE_DELTA post-flip holdout
status: INTERIM — near band only, n too small to score. Not a verdict. See openItems.
priorRecon: docs/probe-data/soda-tb-control-recon.json (2026-07-15; its hit_rate_core_test was INCONCLUSIVE — this is the first actual count)

clock.caveat: single anchor — the skill's hardened rule wants >=4 anchors across the fight; game-clock drift vs video (~2s) is NOT yet corrected here

design.why: Her Hit Rate ▲38.91% (>=20 Golden Chips) is a BURST-slot effect, so every HR window sits inside a Full Burst. But the boss band script (docs/data/range-data.md) puts each of her 5 burst windows cleanly inside exactly ONE band, and the gaps between them give an HR-OFF control in the same band, same fight, same unit.
design.gateCheck: all 5 bursts clear the >=20-chip gate on the PRE-consume count (50/44/40/38/31 per the 2026-07-15 chip trace), so the HR buff fires on every one
design.bandScript: fightClock: mid 0-33, near 33-70, far 70-106, midfar 106-144, near 144-176, midfar 176-180

countingRule.core: RED numerals with 'CORE HIT' above (crit+core = red + crit icon)
countingRule.denominator: every distinguishable popup numeral in the crosshair region of the sampled frame
countingRule.sampling: 1 frame every 1.2 s (popup persistence ~0.4-0.5 s, so no popup is counted twice)
countingRule.excluded: red VFX blooms with no numerals, the red low-ammo counter box, red laser sights, blue/cyan shield hits, the FB countdown

near_HR0_OFF.note: immediately BEFORE her 5th burst — same band, same fight phase; partly inside helm's Full Burst, which matches FB state without granting her Hit Rate

modelComparison.note: predictions recomputed from src/engine/sg-geometry.ts at the shipped frozen params
modelComparison.interimRead: ON 0.061 misses the cone's 0.160 by 0.099 — INSIDE the +-0.12 tolerance, so the holdout does NOT fire its STOP trigger on this count — and sits 0.009 from the fallback's 0.070. The 95% CI [0.021, 0.165] covers BOTH models, so this does not discriminate them yet.

confounds: DENOMINATOR UNCERTAINTY is the dominant error: popups overlap into stacks (the same value printed 5-7x on top of itself). Counts of distinguishable instances carry roughly +-30%; at 3/40 the fraction is 0.075, at 3/60 it is 0.050.

Value attribution is NOT clean: several popups in the ON window exceed the per-pellet band (e.g. 1029147, 1836099) and are plausibly her skill/rider hits, not pellets. They inflate the denominator; the numerator (cores) is unaffected unless a skill hit itself cored.

The ON window is inside her own Full Burst by construction (the HR buff is burst-gated), so ON frames carry heavy gold VFX while some OFF frames do not. If gold VFX hides red numerals, ON is biased LOW — i.e. the direction that would make the cone look worse. Not quantified.
The in-combat damage counter is TEAM TOTAL, so the per-shot lattice method (the SG gold standard on solo footage) is unavailable here — popup counting is the only route on this recording.
observations: Buff-state value tiers move fast in the ON window (white 218079 -> 229583 -> 218495) and the crit/plain ratio reads 1.373-1.377 in FB but 1.483-1.878 outside it — worth a separate look, not chased here.

One clean core popup gave core/plain = 357129/229583 = 1.5556 while crit/plain = 316027/229583 = 1.3765 in the same frame. Under a purely additive major bracket with base M those imply inconsistent M (1.328 from crit, ~0.74 core bonus from core). Recorded as an OBSERVATION against the 2026-07-22 additive crit/core ruling — single frame, values partly overlapped, NOT a challenge to it.

stepThreeCoupling.finding: These same frames carry the SG LANDING measurement, which is the rebuild target of the engine plan's step 3 (BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC on the aim circle). One popup = one landed pellet is established (sg-pellet-landing.json part 1: no value-merging, off-boss pellets render nothing, dropout factor 1.0 [1,1.3]), so popups per shot / 10 IS the landing fraction. The marginal cost is reading the ammo counter, which is in frame in every sample already taken.
stepThreeCoupling.whyItMatters: Four candidate near-band landing numbers disagree by ~50%: live table 0.888, geo static BAND_SG_HIT_FRAC 0.797, pelletLandFrac Gaussian 0.922, and the only direct measurement (drake popupcount) 0.600. Dropout cannot reconcile 0.600 with 0.888 (0.600 x 1.3 = 0.78). The live table was calibrated against the OLD flat-0.85 core model (stated in sim.ts); the cone replaced the core half in 2026-07-19 and the landing half was never re-derived.
stepThreeCoupling.uniqueToThisFootage: her ▲38.91 windows make this the only SG recording with a Hit-Rate toggle inside ONE fight, so it tests whether landing is HR-dependent at all — live table says no; the Gaussian form predicts near 0.922 -> 0.998 (+8% relative).
stepThreeCoupling.byProductEstimate: NOT RELIABLE, recorded only to show the signal is there: popups per sampled frame averaged ~7.2 at near in both arms (ON 10/6/5/8/7/7, OFF 9/5/9/6). Shot cadence from the ammo counter is a solid 0.6 s/shot in both arms. But popup persistence (~0.4-0.5 s) aliases against that cadence, and reload-gap frames were excluded, so popups-per-FRAME is a biased estimator of pellets-per-SHOT in both directions. A real number needs per-shot 60 fps stepping (the coreband2 method), not this sampling.
stepThreeCoupling.guardrail: Per-unit SG landing is CLOSED (owner override 2026-07-17, open-questions A31 (U17): class table stands, per-unit refit REJECTED). Any landing number from this footage is a CLASS-table / band-shape check and a step-3 geometry target — never a per-unit value for soda-twinkling-bunny.

openItems: PROTOCOL UPGRADE: re-run as a JOINT pass — per band, step 60 fps across individual shots and record (a) popups per shot = landed pellets (step 3) and (b) cores among them (step 1). Same frames, both deliverables, and it fixes the denominator problem that limits the core count.
Extend the near-band count to n>=100 per arm (roughly 12 more frames per arm) before scoring.
Count the other three bands: mid (burst 1, video 12-27), far (burst 3, 84-99), midfar (burst 4, 126-141), each against its adjacent HR-off gap.
Re-anchor the clock on >=4 timer reads across the fight (the hardened rule) before the band edges are trusted to the second.
Second recording available for replication: docs/probes/720-kit-audit/soda tb.MP4 (2026-07-21, liter/crown/soda-twinkling-bunny/helm).
